# Private Redaction

> A free web tool to redact sensitive information from documents in your browser, with cryptographic proof that the AI processing stayed private.

Private Redaction blacks out sensitive details — names, emails, phone numbers, addresses, government IDs, card numbers, and any custom terms — in PDF, DOCX and TXT files. The document is opened and redacted entirely on the user's own device; the file is never uploaded. To find what is sensitive, the extracted text is analysed by Nillion's nilAI, a private LLM running inside an AMD SEV-SNP Trusted Execution Environment (TEE). Every run is verified against the enclave's hardware attestation and the response signature, so a user can confirm the processing was genuinely private rather than taking it on trust.

## Key facts
- Free, no sign-up, runs client-side in the browser.
- Input: PDF, DOCX, TXT, MD. Output: a redacted PDF with the sensitive content truly removed, not just visually covered.
- Privacy: the file stays on the device; the extracted text is sent straight from the browser to the Nillion TEE using a short-lived delegation token, so it does not pass through the app's own server. (If the direct path is unavailable it falls back to a stateless relay; the UI shows which path each run used.)
- Verifiable: displays an AMD SEV-SNP attestation result and a per-response signature check.
- Built on Nillion nilAI (private LLM inference in a TEE).

## Links
- App: https://privateredact.app/
- Terms of Use: https://privateredact.app/terms.html
- Nillion private LLMs: https://docs.nillion.com/build/private-llms/overview
- About Nillion: https://nillion.com
